auditors.finding: The repo already provides readonly packet-owned runtime attestation detail through status --json and healthcheck --json, but path-bearing/internal fields and browser-owned super-verdict synthesis remain out of bounds.

Repo evidence supports a stricter reading: the dedicated design-package screen and contour queue still describe runtime attestation detail as needing a bounded attestation packet, so a future implementation must stay limited to a safe subset and exclude path-bearing/internal detail.
No substantive canon drift was found in the spec, matrix, or audit. The only block was pre-commit closeout overclaim before the contour had actually been committed and pushed.

evidence.fact: The Runtime Status screen is defined as status --json with optional operator-triggered healthcheck detail, with explicit attestation fields and required separation of desired vs effective mode.
Live attestation is owned by healthcheck --json, while status --json may expose only summary and must not replace live attestation.
COMMAND_API distinguishes delegated status truth from healthcheck owner truth and explicitly defines stable_runtime_consumer plus deterministic_stable_recovery_contract/result surfaces.
status --json already emits attestation_summary and stable_runtime_consumer.
healthcheck --json already emits top-level deterministic_stable_recovery_contract and deterministic_stable_recovery_result.
CLI tests verify readonly stable_runtime_consumer contract detail inside status --json.
CLI tests verify status --json delegates deterministic_stable_recovery_result as nested secondary detail rather than top-level owner truth.
CLI tests verify healthcheck owner-path recovery result, including effectful_claim_allowed and guardrail_status distinction between confirmed and observation_only.
CLI tests verify current_proxy_url remains a separate truth surface from nested proxy candidate evidence.
UI shell requires structured attestation_summary fields and treats them as validated packet-owned runtime snapshot data.
The design-package screen passport for runtime attestation detail remains admission-required and warns that runtime attestation needs bounded JSON packet truth.

checks.reason: Existing status and healthcheck packets already provide readonly attestation detail surfaces.
Artifacts preserve status --json as primary operator truth and healthcheck --json as deeper owner-path attestation detail.
Artifacts keep deterministic_stable_recovery_result secondary and forbid collapse into a stronger runtime-health claim.
Artifacts explicitly exclude selected_source_path and other implementation/file-location detail.

A stricter deferred reading remains plausible for the full design-package screen, but existing status/healthcheck packets are sufficient for readonly admission of a bounded safe subset without requiring a new packet.
